title: RichText column
excerpt: We really need a RichText column, that can except more than 2000 characters and in which we can format the text any which way we want. NOT a separate work doc, or anything like that. I’m sure there are a plethora of use …

posts.author: wesoos (Wessel Oosthuizen)
posts.cookedHtml: We really need a RichText column, that can except more than 2000 characters and in which we can format the text any which way we want. NOT a separate work doc, or anything like that. I’m sure there are a plethora of use cases and people needing this type of column.

posts.author: JoseDaher (Jose Daher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree!

Or this, or a “markdown” column.

The doc column forces you to open the file to see the content. It’s not convenient.

Happy new year!

posts.author: monica46cole (Monica Cole)
posts.cookedHtml:
I hear you, José. Having a RichText column that allows for more than 2000 characters and offers extensive formatting options directly within a platform can be incredibly useful. This would eliminate the need to use external documents and streamline the process of writing and editing content.

Whether it’s for detailed project descriptions, creative writing, or comprehensive documentation, such a feature would indeed cater to a variety of use cases. It’s a great suggestion that I’m sure many users would appreciate.

Have you considered reaching out to the platform’s support or feedback channels to share this suggestion? Sometimes, user feedback can drive new features and improvements https://www-homedepotsurvey.com
